title: IELTS General Training Mock Test 2
description: Complete IELTS General Training test with all 4 skills
instructions: Read the instructions for each part of the paper carefully. Answer all the questions. Write your answers in the answer sheet. You must complete the answer sheet within the time limit. At the end of the test, hand in both this question paper and your answer sheet.

words.example: Metropolitan areas have millions of residents.
Cities need good infrastructure.
Many people commute to the city center.

words.definition: A place to live or stay
words.example: Finding accommodation in big cities is expensive.

words.definition: The business of travel and leisure
words.example: Tourism is important for the local economy.
The restaurant serves authentic Italian food.
Thai cuisine is known for its bold flavors.
I made a reservation for dinner.

words.definition: The latest time to complete something
words.example: The deadline for the project is Friday.
I discussed the issue with my colleague.
She received a promotion at work.
Networking is important for career growth.
He bought a fast food franchise.
They plan to renovate the old building.
The apartment has spacious rooms.
The sports facilities are excellent.
Regular maintenance is required.
We need sustainable solutions.
The city has a diverse population.
The downtown area is vibrant at night.

vocabList.definition: A place to live or stay
The business of travel and leisure

content.text: Cities around the world are growing rapidly. By 2050, it is estimated that nearly 70% of the world's population will live in urban areas. This rapid urbanization brings both opportunities and challenges. Metropolitan areas offer better employment prospects, healthcare, and education, but they also face problems like traffic congestion and housing shortages.

One of the biggest challenges for modern cities is transportation. Many people commute long distances to work every day. Public transport systems like buses, trains, and metros help reduce the number of cars on the road. However, building and maintaining these systems requires significant investment. Some cities have introduced bike-sharing programs and pedestrian zones to encourage greener forms of transport.

Housing is another major concern. As more people move to cities, the demand for accommodation increases. This drives up rental and property prices. Some cities are responding by building more affordable housing and renovating old buildings. The concept of 'mixed-use' development, where residential and commercial spaces are combined, is becoming more popular.

Tourism is a double-edged sword for cities. On one hand, it brings money and creates jobs. On the other hand, it can lead to overcrowding and damage to historic buildings. Many popular destinations are now implementing strategies to manage tourist numbers and protect their cultural heritage.

Technology is transforming how cities function. Smart cities use data and sensors to manage traffic, energy use, and public services. For example, smart traffic lights can adjust their timing based on traffic flow, reducing congestion. Energy-efficient buildings are becoming standard in new construction projects.

Despite the challenges, cities continue to attract people from rural areas and other countries. This migration brings cultural diversity and economic dynamism. Cities that successfully manage their growth while maintaining quality of life will thrive in the coming decades.

Community involvement is crucial for urban development. Local residents should have a voice in planning decisions that affect their neighborhoods. Many cities now hold public consultations and use online platforms to gather feedback from citizens before implementing major projects.

The future of cities depends on sustainable planning. This means balancing economic growth with environmental protection and social equity. Cities that invest in green spaces, renewable energy, and inclusive development will be better prepared for the challenges of the 21st century.

subTasks.instruction: Which paragraph (A-H) contains the following information?

questions.text: How cities are using technology to improve services
The impact of tourism on historic sites
Problems with finding affordable housing
Community participation in city planning
Environmental considerations for future development
Transportation challenges in urban areas
Population growth predictions
Benefits of cultural diversity in cities
What percentage of people will live in cities by 2050?
What is 'mixed-use' development?

subTasks.instruction: Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.

questions.text: Public transport systems are cheap to build and maintain.
Smart traffic lights can help reduce traffic jams.
Rental prices are the highest in European cities.

content.text: Effective communication in the workplace is essential for success. Whether you are speaking with colleagues, managers, or clients, clear communication helps prevent misunderstandings and builds professional relationships.

One of the most important skills is active listening. This means fully concentrating on what is being said rather than just passively hearing the message. When you actively listen, you ask clarifying questions and summarize what you heard to ensure understanding.

Written communication is equally important. Emails should be clear, concise, and professional. Always check your spelling and grammar before sending. Use a professional tone, especially when corresponding with senior colleagues or external contacts.

Meetings are a common part of workplace life. To make meetings productive, come prepared with an agenda and any necessary materials. During the meeting, stay focused on the topics being discussed and contribute constructively.

Networking involves building relationships with people in your industry. This can lead to new opportunities, collaborations, and career advancement. Attend industry events, join professional organizations, and maintain contact with former colleagues.
Handling conflict professionally is another crucial skill. When disagreements arise, address them directly but respectfully. Focus on finding solutions rather than assigning blame.

subTasks.instruction: Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS.

questions.text: What does active listening involve besides hearing?
What must you check before you send an email?
What should be prepared before a meeting starts?

subTasks.instruction: Answer the questions using NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS.

questions.text: What does clear communication help prevent at work?
What does active listening require a person to be on the speaker?
What two qualities should emails have?
What can networking help an employee achieve?
What should be avoided when handling conflict?

subTasks.instruction: Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.

questions.text: Summarizing helps ensure you understood the speaker.
You should use a casual tone when emailing external contacts.
Productive meetings require staying focused on the agenda.
Most professional organizations have high membership fees.
Conflict should only be handled by managers.

content.text: Food is an important part of culture and identity. Different countries and regions have their own unique cuisines, which reflect their history, climate, and available ingredients.

Italian cuisine, for example, varies by region. Northern Italy is known for rice dishes and rich sauces, while southern Italy is famous for its pasta and olive oil. The Mediterranean diet, which includes olive oil, vegetables, and fish, is considered one of the healthiest in the world.

Asian cuisines offer incredible diversity. Chinese food varies from region to region, with Sichuan cuisine known for its spicy dishes and Cantonese food for its dim sum. Japanese cuisine emphasizes fresh ingredients and artistic presentation, while Thai food is famous for balancing sweet, sour, salty, and spicy flavors.

Street food is an essential part of food culture in many countries. It offers authentic, affordable meals and provides income for millions of people worldwide. From tacos in Mexico to pad thai in Thailand, street food reflects local tastes and traditions.
Food also brings people together. Family meals, celebrations, and festivals often center around food. Sharing a meal is a universal way to build relationships and strengthen community bonds.

subTasks.instruction: Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.

questions.text: Cuisine is influenced by the weather of a region.
The Mediterranean diet prohibits the consumption of meat.
Street food is generally very expensive.
Japanese food focuses on how the dish looks.

subTasks.instruction: Match the cuisine to the characteristic.

questions.text: Known for balancing four distinct flavors.
Famous for dim sum.
Associated with rice and rich sauces in the north.
Known for highly spicy dishes.

subTasks.instruction: Complete the summary. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the text.

questions.text: A region's cuisine is a reflection of its history and ______.
Southern Italy is specifically known for its use of ______.
Japanese cooking places high importance on using ______.
Street food provides a source of ______ for many people.
Street food in Mexico is represented by ______.
Sharing food is a method used worldwide to build ______.

sections.title: Task 1: Letter
sections.subtitle: Part 1: Writing
sections.instruction: You should spend about 20 minutes on this task. Write at least 150 words.
sections.prompt: You have lost something in a taxi and want to find it. Write a letter to the taxi company. In your letter: explain what you lost, describe where you were sitting in the taxi, suggest how the item can be returned to you.
bullets: Describe where you were sitting in the taxi
Suggest how the item can be returned to you

sections.title: Task 2: Essay
sections.instruction: You should spend about 40 minutes on this task. Write at least 250 words.
sections.prompt: Some people believe that unpaid community service should be a compulsory part of high school programs. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
bullets: Include relevant examples from your knowledge or experience

listening.title: Listening
listening.instructions: You will hear a number of different recordings and you will have to answer questions on what you hear. There will be time for you to read the instructions and questions, and you will have a chance to check your work. All the recordings will be played once only. The test is in 4 parts. At the end of the test, you will be given 10 minutes to transfer your answers to the answer sheet.

sections.title: Hotel Reservation
sections.subtitle: Part 1: Listening
sections.description: You will hear a conversation between a customer and a hotel receptionist. First, you have some time to look at questions 1-10.
sections.transcript:
Receptionist: Good afternoon, Grand Hotel. How may I help you?

Customer: Good afternoon. I'd like to make a reservation, please.

Receptionist: Certainly. May I have your name?

Customer: Yes, it's Michael Johnson. M-I-C-H-A-E-L J-O-H-N-S-O-N.

Receptionist: Thank you, Mr. Johnson. How many nights will you be staying?

Customer: I'll be staying for three nights.

Receptionist: Three nights. And what type of room would you prefer?

Customer: A double room, please.

Receptionist: A double room. And when will you be arriving?

Customer: We're planning to arrive on the 22nd of March.

Receptionist: 22nd March. Do you have any special requests?

Customer: Yes, we'd prefer a quiet room if possible, away from the main road.

Receptionist: I understand. We can definitely arrange a quiet room. Now, the price includes breakfast each morning. Check-in is from 2 pm onwards.

Customer: That's great. Is there parking available at the hotel?

Receptionist: Yes, we have private parking available for guests at no additional charge.

Customer: Perfect. Could you give me the hotel's phone number in case I need to contact you?

Receptionist: Of course. Our number is 0171 555 8922.

Receptionist: 0171 555 8922. And how will you be paying for the reservation?

Customer: I'll pay by credit card.

Receptionist: Excellent. I'll confirm your reservation via email shortly. Thank you for choosing Grand Hotel, Mr. Johnson. We look forward to welcoming you.

Customer: Thank you. Goodbye.
sections.context: Conversation between two speakers - social situation

subTasks.instruction: Complete the form below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

questions.text: Guest name:
Number of nights:
Room type:
Arrival date:
Special request:

subTasks.instruction: Answer the questions below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.

questions.text: What is included in the price?
What time is check-in?
Is parking available?
What is the hotel's phone number?
How will the guest pay?

sections.title: City Tour Introduction
sections.subtitle: Part 2: Listening
sections.description: You will hear a guide giving information about a city tour. First, you have some time to look at questions 11-20.
sections.transcript:
Guide: Good morning, everyone. Welcome to our city walking tour. My name is Sarah, and I'll be your guide today. Before we set off, let me give you some important information about the tour.

Our tour starts from the Tourist Information Office, which is located just opposite the main train station. Please meet there 10 minutes before the scheduled departure time. The walking tour lasts approximately two hours, so please wear comfortable shoes as you'll be on your feet for most of the time.

The price per person is £25, which includes entrance fees to all the historic sites we will visit. Lunch and drinks are not included, but we will stop at a nice café where you can purchase refreshments.

The tour runs on certain days of the week - specifically Wednesdays, Fridays, and Sundays. We don't operate daily, so please check the schedule when booking. We recommend booking at least 24 hours in advance to secure your spot, especially during peak season.

Our maximum group size is 15 people, ensuring a more intimate and personalized experience. The tour is available in multiple languages, including English, French, Spanish, and German. Please let us know your language preference when booking.

During the tour, you will see several famous landmarks. We will visit the historic cathedral, which dates back to the 12th century, and the old castle, which now houses a museum. Unfortunately, we don't include the modern sports stadium in our route, as it's located outside the city centre.

Please bring a camera to capture the beautiful scenery, and don't forget to bring an umbrella in case of rain. If you have any questions, feel free to ask me now. Let's begin our tour!

subTasks.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.
subTasks.text: The tour starts from
How long does the walking tour last?
What is included in the tour price?
What should visitors bring?
When does the tour run?
How much does the tour cost per person?
What is the maximum group size?
Which landmark is not included in the tour?
What language options are available?
How far in advance should visitors book?

sections.title: Discussion about Project
sections.subtitle: Part 3: Listening
sections.description: You will hear a discussion between a student and supervisor about a research project. First, you have some time to look at questions 21-30.
sections.transcript:
Supervisor: Come in, Emma. Please take a seat. I've read through your project proposal.

Student: Thank you, Dr. Brown. I'm nervous about this project. I hope it's on the right track.

Supervisor: It's a good start, but I think we need to strengthen it. Your topic on water conservation is very relevant. However, I feel you should add some statistical data to support your arguments.

Student: Statistical data? What kind do you mean?

Supervisor: Things like water usage figures, population statistics, and any existing research findings. Numbers make arguments more convincing. Also, how many interviews are you planning to conduct?

Student: I was thinking of around 15 to 20 interviews.

Supervisor: That's a reasonable number. Where do you plan to conduct these interviews?

Student: I thought I'd go to the local community - maybe residential areas and schools.

Supervisor: Good idea. Getting real perspectives from the community will add depth to your research. Now, when is your first draft due?

Student: It's due next Friday.

Supervisor: That's only a few days away! You need to work on that quickly. Also, the minimum word count is 3000 words, so make sure you meet that requirement.

Student: 3000 words. I'll make sure to write enough. Dr. Brown, what software should I use for analyzing the data?

Supervisor: I recommend using SPSS - it's excellent for statistical analysis. It will help you process your survey results efficiently.

Student: I'll download SPSS right away. When is the final presentation?

Supervisor: The final presentation is scheduled for next month. After I approve the final report, you'll present your findings to the class.

Student: Thank you for all your help, Dr. Brown. I feel much more confident now.

Supervisor: You're welcome, Emma. Just remember to submit the draft on time. Good luck!
sections.context: Conversation between two speakers - academic context

subTasks.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.
subTasks.text: What is the main topic of the project?
What does the supervisor suggest adding?
When is the first draft due?
What is the minimum word count required?
Where should the student conduct the survey?
What software is recommended for analysis?
How many interviews are required?

subTasks.instruction: Answer the questions below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.

questions.text: What is the title of the project?
Who will approve the final report?
When is the final presentation?

sections.title: Lecture on Renewable Energy
sections.subtitle: Part 4: Listening
sections.description: You will hear a lecture about renewable energy sources. First, you have some time to look at questions 31-40.
sections.transcript:
Professor: Good afternoon, class. Today we'll be discussing renewable energy sources and their role in our future energy needs. This is an increasingly important topic as we try to reduce our carbon emissions and move towards more sustainable energy solutions.

Let's start with solar energy. Solar energy comes from the sun, and we capture it using photovoltaic panels. These panels convert sunlight into electricity. Solar power is particularly abundant in sunny regions and can be used to generate electricity for homes and businesses.

Wind energy is another rapidly growing renewable source. Wind turbines convert wind energy into electricity. They work by using the wind to turn large blades, which then spin a generator to produce electricity. One of the main advantages of wind energy is that it produces no pollution during operation.

Hydroelectric power is one of the oldest forms of renewable energy. It uses flowing water to generate electricity, typically by damming rivers. Hydroelectric power has the highest efficiency among renewable sources - it can convert over 90% of the energy from flowing water into electricity. Countries like Norway and Brazil rely heavily on hydroelectric power.

Geothermal energy comes from the heat stored beneath the Earth's surface. This heat can be used directly for heating buildings or to generate electricity. Countries like Iceland are leaders in using geothermal energy for both heating and electricity generation.

Finally, biomass energy is derived from organic materials such as agricultural waste, wood, and even municipal waste. When burned, these materials release energy that can be used for heating or electricity generation. However, biomass is considered carbon-neutral because the plants absorbed carbon dioxide while growing.

Now, let's talk about challenges. The main challenge for renewable energy is storage. Unlike fossil fuels, renewable energy sources are often intermittent - the sun doesn't always shine, and the wind doesn't always blow. Developing efficient and cost-effective energy storage solutions is crucial for the widespread adoption of renewables.

Germany is currently a leader in solar energy adoption, with the highest solar panel capacity in Europe. They have invested heavily in solar technology and now generate a significant portion of their electricity from solar power.

In conclusion, renewable energy offers many benefits, including reduced pollution and energy security. However, we need to address the challenges of storage and cost to make these sources more widely available. Thank you for your attention.

subTasks.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.

questions.text: Solar energy comes from the:
Wind turbines convert wind energy into:
Hydroelectric power uses flowing:
Geothermal energy comes from:
Biomass energy is derived from:

subTasks.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.
subTasks.text: Which renewable source has the highest efficiency?
What is the main advantage of wind energy?
Which country is a leader in solar energy?
What is the main challenge for renewable energy?

subTasks.instruction: Match the energy source with its primary use. Write the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

questions.text: Solar panels -

parts.title: Part 1: Introduction and Interview
parts.instruction: Answer the examiner's questions about familiar topics. Speak naturally and give full answers.

questions.text: What form of transport do you most often use?
Do you prefer public or private transport? (Why?)
How could transport be improved in your city?
Did you learn to ride a bike as a child?

parts.title: Part 2: Long Turn
parts.instruction: You will have 1 minute to prepare, then speak for 1-2 minutes on the topic.

topicCard.topic: Describe a skill you learned when you were a child.
bulletPoints: explain why this skill was useful

roundingQuestions.text: Do you think children should learn practical skills at school?
What other skills are important for children to learn?

parts.title: Part 3: Discussion
parts.instruction: Discuss more abstract topics related to Part 2. Give reasons and examples.

questions.text: What skills are important for success in the modern workplace?
Should companies train their employees or hire experienced workers?
How has technology changed the way we learn new skills?
Do you think traditional skills are still valuable in today's world?
